summary: Key extracted metrics: Incident response SLA = P1 (Critical) Data center outage, data breach risk <=15 minutes <=4 hours; Incident response SLA = P2 (High) System performance degradation affecting >30% users <=30 minutes <=8 hours; Incident response SLA = P3 (Medium) Minor system issues, no user impact <=2 hours <=24 hours; Incident response SLA = P4 (Low) Documentation updates, routine maintenance <=24 hours <=7 business days; Audit log retention = minimum 180 days; Liquidated damages = 20% of total contract value; Confidentiality survival period = 3 years; Data retention limit = no copies after service completion; Local-only data processing = on-premises only; no cloud/off-site transfer.

review_matrix.evidence: 5. Data Breach and Security Incident Notification
review_matrix.evidenceAnchor: 5. Data Breach and Security Incident Notification
review_matrix.recommendation: Maintain this clause and keep evidence traceable.

review_matrix.evidence: Party A has the right to audit Party B's data processing activities, security controls, access logs, subcontractor activity, and personnel access records upon reasonable notice. Party B shall provide relevant evidence and remediate confirmed findings within the agreed remediation period.
review_matrix.evidenceAnchor: Party A has the right to audit Party B's data processing activities, security controls, access logs, subcontractor activity, and personnel access records upon reasonable notice. Party B shall provide relevant evidence and remediate confirmed findings within the agreed remediation period.
review_matrix.recommendation: Maintain this clause and keep evidence traceable.

review_matrix.evidence: P1 (Critical) Data center outage, data breach risk <=15 minutes <=4 hours
review_matrix.evidenceAnchor: P1 (Critical) Data center outage, data breach risk <=15 minutes <=4 hours
review_matrix.recommendation: Verify owner, clock-start definition, escalation path, and evidence trail.

review_matrix.evidence: P2 (High) System performance degradation affecting >30% users <=30 minutes <=8 hours
review_matrix.evidenceAnchor: P2 (High) System performance degradation affecting >30% users <=30 minutes <=8 hours
review_matrix.recommendation: Verify owner, clock-start definition, escalation path, and evidence trail.

review_matrix.evidence: P3 (Medium) Minor system issues, no user impact <=2 hours <=24 hours
review_matrix.evidenceAnchor: P3 (Medium) Minor system issues, no user impact <=2 hours <=24 hours
review_matrix.recommendation: Verify owner, clock-start definition, escalation path, and evidence trail.

review_matrix.evidence: P4 (Low) Documentation updates, routine maintenance <=24 hours <=7 business days
review_matrix.evidenceAnchor: P4 (Low) Documentation updates, routine maintenance <=24 hours <=7 business days
review_matrix.recommendation: Verify owner, clock-start definition, escalation path, and evidence trail.

review_matrix.evidence: Audit Logs: Party B must maintain complete access logs for all data operations for a minimum of 180 days.
review_matrix.evidenceAnchor: Audit Logs: Party B must maintain complete access logs for all data operations for a minimum of 180 days.
review_matrix.recommendation: Preserve the audit-log retention requirement and define inspector, export format, and tamper-evidence controls.

review_matrix.evidence: In the event of a breach of confidentiality by Party B, Party B shall pay liquidated damages equal to 20% of the total contract value. Party B shall also compensate Party A for direct and indirect losses caused by the breach.
review_matrix.evidenceAnchor: In the event of a breach of confidentiality by Party B, Party B shall pay liquidated damages equal to 20% of the total contract value. Party B shall also compensate Party A for direct and indirect losses caused by the breach.
review_matrix.recommendation: Review whether the damages cap covers the sensitivity and business value of the protected data.

review_matrix.evidence: Party B shall not disclose Confidential Information to third parties without Party A's written consent. Party B shall return or securely destroy Confidential Information upon termination. Confidentiality obligations survive the termination of this agreement for a period of 3 years.
review_matrix.evidenceAnchor: Party B shall not disclose Confidential Information to third parties without Party A's written consent. Party B shall return or securely destroy Confidential Information upon termination. Confidentiality obligations survive the termination of this agreement for a period of 3 years.
review_matrix.recommendation: Confirm whether three years is sufficient for the protected data classes.

review_matrix.evidence: Data Retention: Party B may not retain any copies of Party A's data after service completion.
review_matrix.evidenceAnchor: Data Retention: Party B may not retain any copies of Party A's data after service completion.
review_matrix.recommendation: Preserve the no-copy requirement and define deletion evidence.

review_matrix.evidence: Data Localization: All Party A's data must be processed and stored exclusively on Party A's on-premises servers. No data may be transferred to any cloud service or off-site location.
review_matrix.evidenceAnchor: Data Localization: All Party A's data must be processed and stored exclusively on Party A's on-premises servers. No data may be transferred to any cloud service or off-site location.
review_matrix.recommendation: Preserve the local-only processing control and define exceptions approval.

risks: Liquidated damages are fixed at 20% of total contract value; check whether this cap is sufficient for customer PII, financial records, and internal business data. Evidence: "In the event of a breach of confidentiality by Party B, Party B shall pay liquidated damages equal to 20% of the total contract value. Party B shall also compensate Party A for direct and indirect losses caused by the breach."
action_items: Preserve the audit-log retention requirement and define inspector, export format, and tamper-evidence controls.
Review whether the damages cap covers the sensitivity and business value of the protected data.
Confirm whether three years is sufficient for the protected data classes.
Preserve the no-copy requirement and define deletion evidence.
Preserve the local-only processing control and define exceptions approval.
Verify that each P1-P4 SLA has an owner, clock-start definition, escalation path, and evidence trail.
